

Module 4: Pre-Launch Sequence

Module 4: Pre-Launch Sequence

Sacred Cows with Sour Milk

Subject Lines That Work Well in the Pre-Launch:

Direct Response 2.0 - Questions to Explore:

Themeplates

Themeplate 6: Slaughterhouse V (MVF 3-5)

THEMEPLATE:

EXAMPLE:

Themeplate 7 - “Slaughterhouse IV”

THEMEPLATE:

EXAMPLE

Pre-Launch Checklist:



Sacred Cows with Sour Milk



These are the conventional and commonly held, but oppressive beliefs that derail the progress of your market: 



ie. 

I can’t build an online business until I learn how to use Wordpress

I can’t play the guitar until I learn how to read sheet music

In order to have a 6-figure business I need to have a massive list 

I can’t learn how to speak French because I’m terrible at memorizing verbs

I can’t meditate because I can’t quiet my mind



Subject Lines That Work Well in the Pre-Launch:



The best subject lines for the Pre-Launch are curiosity driven and imply a new “diagnosis”. 



i’ll be the first to admit it… 

What you may not know about (opportunity) 

Keep this between us

Why ____ doesn’t work. 

For serious…

5 Reasons Why You’re Still (experiencing problem)

What you need to know about (topic)

I can’t believe I once believed this… 

Can we be honest…

You might want to sit down for this. 

You don’t really believe that do you? 

I won’t be winning any friends… 

Can I tell you a secret…

Why isn’t everyone talking about this? 

The REAL reason X

Can’t believe people still believe this… 

I wish I learnt this sooner

Do you really still believe this?

Really? In 2017? 

3 Unexpected Reasons to _______

Why you haven’t been able to ______

3 Reasons Why ______



Direct Response 2.0 - Questions to Explore: 



What about your market’s struggles breaks your heart? 



What reality do you see for them -- that they don’t yet see for themselves? 



What pains you most about their inability to see that potential? 



What’s the biggest excuse your avatar makes for having NOT achieved a favorable outcome yet?



How might that excuse be justified? How can you validate that excuse? 



What is it about you or your method that overcomes that limitation?  



How do your biggest competitors fall short in overcoming it? 



What is a false belief they have about the industry? 



About themselves or their ability/capacity to achieve it?



About the best way to solve the problem or achieve the outcome?



Why might it be a GOOD thing that they’ve waited until now? 



What are their “sacred cows with sour milk”? (these are the most commonly held and widely-accepted beliefs and paradigms that DON’T serve your prospect, and that they’re READY to be released and liberated from if given a better option). 



	SCSM1 (as it relates to the outcome - macro level): 



ie. it’s impossible to make a living as a yoga teacher

Ie. a need a big email list to have a successful online business 



	SCSM2 (as it relates to the obstacles/problems - experiential level):  



	ie. writing copy is a hard and painful process. 



	ie. I’m a yoga teacher, I shouldn’t have to be “sell”. I just want to serve. Shouldn’t I 

     just trust that the people I’m meant to serve will find me? 





	SCSM3 (as it relates to their own abilities - micro/granular level):  



	Ie. I’m terrible at the techy stuff - there’s no way I can manage half a dozen social 

media accounts and all the other “business things” I’d need to do. Can’t someone just 

do all that for me? 





      13. What are the reversals, reframes, or “ahas” that counter those “sacred cows”? 



	“AHA 1” ie. You’re right, you CAN’T make a living as a yoga teacher. But you can 

make an amazing living (and have a great lifestyle) as a “yoga entrepreneur”. Would 

you be open to shifting your definition?



	“AHA 2” ie. How exactly are you being of “service”, when you’re stressing out about 

how you’re going to cover next month’s rent? Would it be possible that TRUE 

SERVICE doesn’t require endless self-sacrifice, but a more harmonious and 

synergistic relationship with money and the business side? 



“AHA 3” ie. While being on EVERY single social channel may be needed for a 

“yoga celebrity”, it’s by no means necessary to have a thriving career as a 

Yoga entrepreneur. Much like your practice - running a successful business doesn’t 

rely on 100 different “poses”, but precision, mindfulness and consistency on the few 

that truly serve you and help you grow.  





SCSM1:

Reframe 1:

SCSM2:

Reframe 2:

SCSM3

Reframe 3:

	

Themeplates



Use the “Slaughterhouse” template for EACH of the reframes you make in your launch. 



In most cases, you’ll be sending out 2-3 of these emails - each addressing their own SCSM. 



Themeplate 6: Slaughterhouse V (MVF 3-5)



About:



Use this template when you’re using ONLY email to drive home the “ahas” and belief shifts. 



This template mirrors the classic PASOP structure (see more in 10X Emails or Joanna’s Epic Post) but integrates the principles of “Sacred Cows” and “Ahas’ that are so vital in the pre-launch phase. 



THEMEPLATE:



[Call out a Sacred Cow]

[Agitate by describing the Sour Milk it produces (the “moment of highest tension”] 

[Propose a new, more empowering (and believable) “aha” that counters it]

[Describe the benefits and opportunity adopting this new belief]

[Open up a NEW problem that relates to their ability to adopt/integrate the new view]



EXAMPLE: 





Subject:

Can yoga teachers really make a living?

can we finally get real about this?



Body:



Alright. 



This might not win me any friends, but it has to be said.



The reason you’re still not making it as a yoga teacher is because… well…



… you’re still trying to make it as a yoga teacher.



Think about that for a sec:



If you’re still scrambling around, running from studio to studio, desperately pleading with every stingy studio owner to pay you a liveable wage that wouldn’t force you into moonlighting as a Starbucks barista…



… then maybe it’s time for a new definition.



One that doesn’t induce those “un-yogic” reactions every time a well-meaning BFF or parent has the audacity to ask you how the business is going.



(I’ve worn the same lulus 37 days in a row. How the hell do you think it’s going!?) 



So if this whole yoga teacher thing is just an endless practice of stress, self-doubt, and stinky yoga pants…



… why wouldn’t you be open to changing the job description?



… one that empowers you to serve on a deeper, more sustainable level?



… one that doesn’t force you into veiled smile every time someone questions your career choices?



… one that actually gives you a sip of this whole “freedom thing” that every yogi with an 

instagram account has been going ape sh*t over for the last half decade? 



The truth is…



There are THOUSANDS of yoga professionals who live this reality each and every day.



But they didn’t get there by submitting to penny-pinching studio owners, or trying to see if the green starbucks apron matches their yoga pants.



They got there by FIRST declaring themselves a “yoga entrepreneur”...



… and then learning the practical business skills and mindset shifts that open the floodgates to a deeper well of abundance and service.



They didn’t rely on mantras.



But the powerful marketing strategies that quickly position them as an in-demand yoga professional who gets to put “hustle” into permanent savasana



“Yoga Teacher” → “Yoga Entrepreneur”.



“Great. I get it. But I suck at all that “marketing” stuff. I once tried to set up a squarespace account and I swear, I nearly broke the internet:



That’s a topic for our next email.



This crazy misperception that you need to be “everywhere’ to have a thriving business.



Stay tuned for another email coming later this week that will show you a much simpler framework for marketing your business.



Till then…



(sign off) 



PS. I actually didn’t even build my website until AFTER I “made it” as a yoga entrepreneur. Instead, I armed myself with 3 Core Strategies that immediately allowed me to work less, earn more,and gain leverage to start building out my business.



(hint: unlike that website “you just need to have” these strategies can be implemented right away with ZERO overhead).



Watch out for them in your inbox on Monday morning.



Themeplate 7 - “Slaughterhouse IV” 

About: 



A lighter version of the template above that you can use when you’re using email to drive people to a pre launch asset (video, workshop, challenge, etc.).



In this case, the email’s job ISN’T to deliver the big shift itself, but to sell consumption of the asset that does. 



Here we mimic the “AIDA” formula (Attention - Interest - Desire - Action) 



THEMEPLATE:



[Grab attention with the Sacred Cow and the “sour milk” it produces] 

[Tease at the “Aha”]

[Build up the benefits of shifting the from the SC to the AHA]

[Point to the video/blog/workshop to get the full scoop]



EXAMPLE



Subject:

I wrote this in 9 minutes… 

How long would this take you to write? 



Body:



I’m here to take a stand for you, Ryan.



You see… 



You’ve been fed this crazy lie that “copywriting” is this scary, esoteric, and super hard thing that the average…



“I just want to get my funnel up and running and make $$” entrepreneur can’t handle. 



And the sheer bull-shittery of this wouldn’t drive me so crazy if it wasn’t having such a crippling effect on your business and sanity…  



… pushing back your launch dates over-and-over again as you desperately try to recruit a member of the only profession where it’s still cool to drink before noon.



… finally launching, but cringing at your conversion rates and starting to question whether or not people even want your “thing”?



… searching for more quick fixes. Those “fill-in-the-blank” templates that take even MORE time and effort to shoehorn your message into than it would to DIYTRY (do it yourself the right way)... or WORSE, investing half your launch budget for a robot or software that can supposedly do it for you.



Sorry. 



Selling is still a sentient job (aka YOUR JOB) 



As well as a…



Fast, fun and muchos profitable one once you know your way around a launch email ;-) 



The SECOND WORKSHOP of our “Profit & Launch” series just went live.



In it, we give you release from endless nights going to war with your macbook pro, and instead show you… 



A clear, simple and effective method for QUICKLY churning out high-converting email copy in a fraction of the time it would take you to hire it out. 



Why you should immediately ditch “copywriting” from your vocabulary… and how to approach the craft in a way that fully leverages your existing talents as a coach, educator, and human.



A powerful go-to “themeplate” (you’ll learn what that is at 14:03 of this video) that can be written in about 15 minutes and DOUBLE your sales on the final day of your launch.



Things are speeding up quickly, and on [X DATE] we’re gonna be releasing the third and FINAL piece of the whole launch & profit puzzle.



But the only way for you to get the FULL benefit from that one is if you take a few mins right now to fully steep in the “major ahas” we have waiting for you here.



Give it a spin -- and don’t forget to leave a comment letting us know how FAST you were able to load up and fire off the closing themeplate (you’ll see what I mean here)



(the current record sits at 9 minutes flat).



Bonus points if you’re cool enough to tell us how many extra SALES it generated ;-) 



Till then…



Peace, Love & Fist Bumps



(sign off)



Pre-Launch Checklist:



Have I coached my prospect on?



Why previous efforts fell short?



Why it wasn’t their fault?



The “sacred cows with sour milk” that sabotaged their success or delayed progress?



Why they should feel renewed hope/optimism that they can do it “this time”?



Why they can trust me to help them succeed this time? 



2 or 3 Major “ahas” or “insights” that now make the desired outcome feel REAL and even PROBABLE. 



The danger in looking elsewhere (ie. how the rest of the industry/competitors are still preaching a flawed approach) 



Do they feel privy to a big secret that the rest of their competitors aren’t?
